message:
fix(vdbe): reject non-unique indexes in multi-join lookup resolver

Second review pass uncovered a correctness bug: the multi-join lookup
fast path took only the *first* matching row from a secondary index
(SeekGE + one Column check + IdxRowid), but for non-unique indexes a
single key value can point at multiple data rows.  For a 1:N join on a
non-unique indexed column, the fast path would silently emit one row
where the correct result set has N rows — a data-loss bug.

The single-join codegen handles this correctly via a SeekGE + Next
duplicate-run loop, but generalising that to N-way chains adds
substantial complexity and is not needed for the bd_zjisk issue #62
query (which joins on INTEGER PRIMARY KEY columns = unique by
definition).

Fix: reject non-unique indexes in `resolve_multi_join_lookup_plan`
so those cases fall back to the nested-scan path, which is slower but
produces the correct row count.  Unique indexes and rowid lookups
(always unique) remain on the fast path.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
